A_collocate_network.interpretation: 1485 distinct H+M × H+M bigrams. 60 strong collocate pairs (PMI > 1.5, ≥3 occurrences). High-PMI pairs represent recurring compound title formulas.

B_initial_vocabulary.interpretation: 77 H+M signs have INITIAL-dominant positional profile (i_rate ≥ 0.50). These constitute the title/determinative vocabulary: professional roles, clan markers, place names. Their top right-neighbors reveal what types of name components follow each title.

C_formula_classification.interpretation: 97 distinct INITIAL signs act as formula-type headers. Cross-tabulation with iconography tests whether different professional titles (different INITIAL signs) appear on seals with different motif types. Systematic divergence = formula type ↔ seal function correlation.

D_polysemy_test.interpretation: 17/21 multi-slot H+M signs show divergent neighbor profiles by positional slot (Roif polysemy test). A sign with high divergence score has systematically different compounds depending on whether it appears in INITIAL, MEDIAL, or TERMINAL position — consistent with shorthand polysemy: same sign, context-dependent meaning.

E_M267_genitive.interpretation: M267 (freq=400): 81% MEDIAL, 83/383 left-neighbors are INITIAL-class (title/determinative) signs. Pattern [INITIAL_sign] · M267 · [X] is consistent with genitive construction '[NAME]-of-[TITLE]' in Dravidian SOV administrative formula. Verdict: GENITIVE_SUPPORTED.

key_findings: A. Collocate network: 1485 H+M×H+M bigrams; 60 strong collocate pairs (PMI>1.5)
B. INITIAL vocabulary: 77 H+M title/determinative signs identified
C. Formula classification: 97 distinct formula types by INITIAL sign
D. Polysemy test: 17/21 multi-slot signs show Roif-predicted divergence
E. M267 genitive: 81% MEDIAL; 83/383 left-neighbors are INITIAL-class → GENITIVE_SUPPORTED
_note: Phase-142: Collocate network, INITIAL vocabulary, formula classification, Roif polysemy test, M267 genitive validation. Real corpus only, no synthetic data.
